# RQ-0849: Consolidate Duplicate Binary Path Validation Logic

## Task Goal

Eliminate the DRY violation in binary path validation by extracting a shared helper function that validates all 7 runner binary paths (`codex_bin`, `opencode_bin`, `gemini_bin`, `claude_bin`, `cursor_bin`, `kimi_bin`, `pi_bin`) and use it in both `validate_config()` and `validate_agent_patch()`.

## Why This Matters

The original implementation had two nearly-identical blocks of binary validation code. This created maintenance risk:
1. When adding a new runner, validation had to be added in two places
2. `kimi_bin` was already missed in `validate_config()` (inconsistent coverage)
3. `pi_bin` was missing from both functions entirely

## Current State (REFINEMENT PHASE)

The consolidation has been completed:
- A shared `validate_agent_binary_paths()` helper exists in `validation.rs` (lines 88-111)
- Both `validate_config()` (line 195) and `validate_agent_patch()` (line 240) now use this helper
- All 7 binary fields are validated consistently: codex_bin, opencode_bin, gemini_bin, claude_bin, cursor_bin, kimi_bin, pi_bin
- Tests for kimi_bin and pi_bin validation have been added to `tests.rs` (lines 143-215)

## Files Modified

1. **`crates/ralph/src/config/validation.rs`** - Contains the shared helper function
2. **`crates/ralph/src/config/tests.rs`** - Contains tests for binary path validation
3. **`crates/ralph/src/config/mod.rs`** - Exports `validate_agent_binary_paths`

## Verification Steps

1. Run `cargo test -p ralph config` to verify all tests pass
2. Run `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` to ensure no warnings
3. Run `make macos-ci` as the final gate before completion

## Expected Outcome

- Both `validate_config()` and `validate_agent_patch()` use the same validation logic
- All 7 binary fields are consistently validated in both functions
- Adding new binary fields in the future requires only updating the helper function
- Duplicate code has been removed
